purpose: Grade Q/A surfacing under the autonomy directive: did the brainstormer ask the right clarifying questions (logged as Q:/A: pairs in ## Assumptions and as event=assumption in the log) and refrain from asking trivia?

evals.name: keep-branch-flag
evals.prompt: Add a `--keep-branch` flag to autonomo that, on bail, suppresses the bail-report `.md` and just leaves the branch silently. The skill is at plugins/autonomo/skills/autonomo/SKILL.md.

expected_questions.q: Should --keep-branch also suppress the structured .log, or only the .md report?
expected_questions.a: Only the .md report — the .log is for tail/post-mortem and cheap to keep.

expected_questions.q: Where does --keep-branch sit in the CLI — before or after the prompt?

acceptable_extras: Whether --keep-branch affects success runs (it should not)
trivia_to_avoid: Whether to use getopts vs manual parsing

evals.name: extract-slugify
evals.prompt: Extract the slug-derivation logic from §3 'Pick workspace' of the autonomo skill into a standalone shell script under scripts/, so we can unit-test it. The skill is at plugins/autonomo/skills/autonomo/SKILL.md.

expected_questions.q: What should the script be named?
Does the SKILL.md still inline the algorithm or just reference the script?
expected_questions.a: Reference only. Inline + script means two sources of truth that drift.

expected_questions.q: How does the script receive input — $1 argv or stdin?
expected_questions.a: $1 argv — matches how the SKILL currently shows the inline form.
scripts/tests/ as a sibling, simple bash runner.

acceptable_extras: Whether the script should set -euo pipefail
Whether to test on macOS bash 3.2 vs newer
trivia_to_avoid: Whether to use printf vs echo

evals.name: last-3-runs-header
evals.prompt: Add a 'last 3 runs' summary block to the `→ /autonomo · run started` header that shows timestamps and outcomes (success / blocked / errored) of the previous three /autonomo runs on this branch. The skill is at plugins/autonomo/skills/autonomo/SKILL.md.

expected_questions.q: Where does the data come from — git log, existing .autonomo/*.log files, or a new state file?
expected_questions.a: Parse from existing .autonomo/*.log files matching the current branch — no new state file.

expected_questions.q: How is each run's outcome derived from its .log?
expected_questions.a: Last meaningful event: event=blocked → blocked; phase=execute event=dispatch_end with PR success → success; neither → errored/incomplete.

expected_questions.q: What if there are fewer than 3 prior runs (or zero)?
expected_questions.a: Show however many exist; if zero, print '(no prior runs on this branch)'.

expected_questions.q: Is 'this branch' matched by branch name in the log, or by the slug-prefixed filename?
expected_questions.a: Branch name (logged in event=run_start branch=…) — slug isn't a stable key across rephrasings.

acceptable_extras: Whether to truncate timestamps to date-only
